The Fallen Caryatid Carrying Her Stone
by Auguste Rodin
France · nineteenth century · 1886–1896 · Bronze
The Burghers of Calais
by Auguste Rodin
Modern sculpture · 1889
Six townsmen walking out to surrender themselves to Edward III to save their city. Rodin rejected the usual heroic pedestal — they stand at ground level, each alone with his own weight.
A Burgher of Calais (Jean d'Aire)
by Auguste Rodin
France · Post-Impressionism · 1889 · Plaster
The Sirens
by Auguste Rodin
France, late 19th-early 20th century · 1889 · bronze
The Fall of the Angels
by Auguste Rodin
France, late 19th-early 20th Century · c. 1890–1900 · marble
Study of Honoré de Balzac
by Auguste Rodin
France, 19th century · 1891–1892 · bronze
The Thinker
by Auguste Rodin
Modern · Rodin · 1880–1904
Originally a small figure above The Gates of Hell — Dante, looking down at his Inferno. Enlarged and cast on its own, it became a universal emblem of thought.
Portrait of Balzac
by Auguste Rodin
France · Post-Impressionism · 1893 · Bronze
The Walking Man
by Auguste Rodin
France · Post-Impressionism · 1895–1905 · Bronze
Head of Arthur Jerome Eddy
by Auguste Rodin
France · nineteenth century · 1898 · Bronze
